question.text: An FPI fund has a total corpus of USD 500 million. A resident Indian entity (eligible fund manager) contributes as a constituent. The fund is an Alternative Investment Fund set up in IFSC. What is the maximum contribution allowed from this resident Indian entity?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** As per the regulations, for an AIF set up in IFSC, the contribution of a resident Indian (other than individuals) who is a fund management entity or its associate shall be up to 10% of the corpus. So maximum = 10% of USD 500 million = USD 50 million. **The Trap:** The 25% limit applies to individual NRI/OCI/RI persons, not to resident Indian entities acting as fund managers in IFSC-based AIFs. **Cross-Exam Context:** NISM Series XIX (Alternative Investment Funds) covers AIF corpus requirements and contribution limits. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** For IFSC-based Retail Schemes, the limit is 10% of AUM instead of 10% of corpus. The base changes but the percentage stays the same.
